title: Implement the first public semantic-to-scene slice
description:
Implement the explicitly approved Design-0002 production path in underwood and underwood_parley, driven only through the external examples/headless crate. Freeze further wind-tunnel expansion until the real path is complete.

## Required Skills
alder,cedar,otter,badger,lynx,rook
design: One coherent patch owns the public document/edit/snapshot/style/preparation/scene façade, pinned Parley adapter, finite-width flow, semantic/hit/caret mapping, retained sibling and paint-only reuse, and honest work counters. No private example shortcut, unsafe, system fonts, stable serialized identity, or proof promotion without the complete gate.
acceptance_criteria: The normative external call site compiles and runs with real pinned-Parley glyphs, bidi and source coverage; old snapshots, sibling reuse, paint-only reuse, width-only stage invalidation, hit/caret/semantics, faithful ligature paint coverage or explicit error, no_std targets, MSRV/all-host CI, rustdoc, and full repository gates pass; Lynx/Rook Must findings are resolved and checked-in evidence remains honest.
notes:
2026-07-21: Bruce Mitchener explicitly approved Design-0002 and directed immediate execution. This authorizes its exact production dependencies, the underwood_parley adapter crate, examples/headless crate, and complete draft foundational public API. No unsafe, publication, stable wire/storage identity, or proof promotion is implied.
Implemented approved Design-0002 public slice: no_std Document/Edit/Snapshot, adapter-owned prepared records, retained LayoutEngine and TextScene, pinned no_std underwood_parley, external mixed Latin/Arabic headless path, real ligature paint clips, snapshot/source/semantic observations, and negative-work assertions. Local product gates pass: example test, workspace tests, clippy -D warnings, rustdoc -D warnings, xtask, x86_64-unknown-none, and wasm32-unknown-unknown. Next: replace/demote synthetic benchmarks so product benchmarks execute these crates.
Replaced the misleading benchmark boundary: synthetic flow/position/text-data/Parley-seam code moved to experiments/ and classified as research tests. Added benches/semantic-scene, which uses only underwood and underwood_parley public APIs across cold, retained, paint-only, width-only, and one-paragraph-edit workloads with WorkReport assertions. Completed Lynx/Rook audit and resolved all Musts: exact topology identity, UTF-8/source validation, charmap fallback, cross-document fragment identity, gap rejection, honest paint coverage, render-complete font instance data, and real benchmark execution. Full local stable/MSRV/no_std/wasm/clippy/test/doc/policy/typos/Beads gates pass.
2026-07-21: Remote CI run 29820819717 passed all eight required jobs on Linux, macOS, and Windows, including formatting/text policy, repository policy, MSRV, rustdoc, no_std portability, clippy, and tests. The checked-in Lynx/Rook review has no unresolved Must findings. The first-semantic-to-scene-spine ledger entry is promoted to Executable; remaining Should/Could limits stay explicit.
close_reason: Design-0002 public semantic-to-scene slice, real public benchmark, adversarial review, local gates, no_std targets, and all eight remote CI jobs are complete; proof is Executable.

title: Establish the dependency-free Underwood core boundary
description:
Create the approved permanent underwood crate boundary without crossing the production-dependency or foundational-public-API gates, enforce no_std portability, and make the first API decision reviewable as one complete packet.

## Required Skills
alder,badger,cedar,shepherd
design: Alder owns the one-crate boundary and private module fences; Badger enforces Cargo and cross-target CI; Cedar specifies the complete draft call site, rustdoc, errors, and naming; Shepherd holds the public API and Parley dependency gates. An empty scaffold is not capability evidence and the ledger must remain honest.
acceptance_criteria: The accepted design records the user's crate authorization; an unpublished dependency-free underwood core crate is registered and checked for x86_64-unknown-none and wasm32-unknown-unknown; no public product item or production dependency is introduced; the API packet includes exact signatures, ownership, errors, complete external call sites, non-goals, and a CHANGELOG migration entry; all repository and Beads gates pass.
notes:
2026-07-21: Started after explicit authorization to begin actual crates. Scope is the real dependency-free no_std underwood package boundary, x86_64-unknown-none/wasm32 proof, and a complete review packet for the still-gated first public API. No production dependency, public product item, unsafe, or stable identity representation is authorized by this claim.
2026-07-21: Added the real unpublished dependency-free underwood no_std core boundary, registered its fence, and added stable x86_64-unknown-none plus wasm32-unknown-unknown CI checks. Both stable target checks pass locally; native workspace MSRV 1.92 passes. An extra local MSRV/bare-metal check was NOT_RUN because that target is not installed for 1.92 and is outside the accepted CI matrix.
2026-07-21: Proposed docs/design/0002-first-public-api-gate.md after Alder/Cedar/Lynx/Rook review. It requests one coherent gate for exact Kurbo/Peniko/Parley dependencies, the complete public call site, owned adapter contract, snapshot-local scene mapping, faithful ligature paint coverage or hard error, concrete errors, invalidation counters, and migration posture. A Must-level cache-identity flaw in the first draft was removed by making LayoutEngine own its immutable paragraph-preparation context.
2026-07-21: Live GitHub ruleset 19313566 now requires the new no_std portability context while preserving linear history, squash-only PRs, merge queue, review, and the prior seven checks. The local full remote audit passes against forest-rs/underwood using the existing credential without storing it. Scheduled audit still requires the separate least-privilege UNDERWOOD_GOVERNANCE_TOKEN human credential gate.
2026-07-21 CI diagnosis: required no_std portability run 29815248202 failed before compiling because dtolnay/rust-toolchain resolved shorthand 1.96 to 1.96.1 and installed targets there, while rust-toolchain.toml made Cargo invoke exact 1.96.0. The locally proven crate/targets were not the failure. Fixed the job to install exact 1.96.0 and taught xtask to enforce that pin.
2026-07-21 validation complete: replacement GitHub Actions run 29815554762 passed all eight required jobs, including exact Rust 1.96.0 x86_64-unknown-none and wasm32-unknown-unknown no_std checks. Live protected-main ruleset 19313566 requires no_std portability. The crate remains unpublished, dependency-free, no_std, and contains no public product item; the complete first-public-API packet remains proposed and gated in docs/design/0002-first-public-api-gate.md.
close_reason: Accepted dependency-free core boundary is implemented and enforced; all local policy gates and required GitHub checks pass without crossing a dependency, public API, or unsafe gate.

title: Design the first permanent semantic-to-scene slice
description:
Turn the accepted charter and ADRs into one narrow but permanent vertical slice from semantic document input through prepared text and scene evidence for the living agent document.

## Required Skills
alder,shepherd
design: Alder defines fences, invariants, crate ownership, and two or three credible slice options. Shepherd stops before new crates, production dependencies, or foundational public APIs. The choice must carry the four accepted experiment obligations instead of bypassing them.
acceptance_criteria: A decision packet names the slice outcome, non-goals, crate and module fences, private experiment boundaries, dependency plan, proof targets, migration posture, alternatives, and the exact human approvals required to implement it.
notes:
2026-07-21: First permanent slice design started on agent/semantic-scene-spine after foundation ratification. No production dependency, unsafe, or permanent public API is assumed by the design work.
2026-07-21: Proposed docs/design/0001-first-semantic-to-scene-slice.md. Recommends one initial no_std underwood façade plus a separately gated underwood_parley production adapter; private modules split only under real dependency/ownership/target pressure. Exact remaining gates: permanent crate shape/creation, immutable Parley pin+features, and reviewable draft public API patch with migration note.
2026-07-21: Bruce explicitly authorized continuous execution and getting started on the actual crates. Applied narrowly to Design-0001 gates 1 and 2: the chosen one-crate dependency-free underwood boundary is accepted and may be created. The production Parley revision/features and first foundational public API remain separate constitutional gates.
close_reason: Design-0001 is accepted: Bruce authorized beginning the actual crates, selecting the one dependency-free underwood core boundary while preserving the separate production-dependency and foundational-public-API gates. Design-0002 and und-oh0.10.1.6 carry the complete next gate.

title: Prove the retained Parley preparation seam
description: Build the public-API conformance corpus for retained analysis, itemization, font selection, shaping, paint slots, break reshaping, vertical text, and inline objects against the accepted Parley policy.
design: Prefer current upstream seams and focused upstream contributions. Any pin or patch must pass ADR-0004 ownership, size, review, dual-run, and removal gates before use.
acceptance_criteria: The conformance matrix executes through Underwood-owned prepared contracts; exact upstream revisions and gaps are recorded; no Parley type leaks into a stable facade; any divergence has an approved lifecycle.
notes:
2026-07-21: Started a private adapter wind tunnel at pinned Parley 45da4a90248b1600277a4294b70d8bfde5ca8e97. Initial executable scope is owned analysis/item records plus copied callback-shaped Latin/Arabic observations, paint-slot separation, font/feature invalidation, and an exact current-main gap matrix. No production pin, patch, permanent adapter crate, or public API is implied.
2026-07-21: Implementation commit fe106d7 adds the private retained-Parley seam wind tunnel. Six tests pass against exact Parley 45da4a90248b1600277a4294b70d8bfde5ca8e97: owned deterministic copy-out of callback-shaped Latin/Arabic records; LTR/RTL and source coverage; weight and kerning invalidation with stable earlier stages; and paint-slot boundaries through an actual Roboto Flex ligature plus Arabic cursive text without physics changes. Current main still lacks retained ShapedText, bounded break reshaping, vertical shaping, core inline objects, and text-data identity. Wider-script, fallback, break, vertical, object, cross-target, and patched-path conformance remain open, so the bead and proof stay Specified.
2026-07-21 commit identity correction: canonical implementation commit is 4b43ca2; it supersedes the unpushed fe106d7 identifier after applying required Taplo manifest formatting. Behavior and evidence are unchanged.

title: Measure text-data distribution tiers
description: Measure the accepted minimal and complex-segmentation inventories in a WebAssembly wind tunnel and prove bundle identity, upgrade invalidation, replay, diagnostics, and licensing.
design: Use a separate wind-tunnel crate after its workspace boundary is approved. Do not introduce a production provider or data dependency while measuring the current Parley compiled path.
acceptance_criteria: Raw, compressed, resident, warm, transient, and throughput evidence is checked in with exact identities; replay and missing-capability traces pass; size-gate outcomes are explicit.
notes:
2026-07-21: Started a separate text-data wind tunnel against immutable Parley commit 45da4a90248b1600277a4294b70d8bfde5ca8e97. Scope is benchmark-only: exact minimal/complex WebAssembly artifacts, compiled-path corpus exercise, identities, replay/diagnostic traces, and honest NOT_RUN fields; no production provider or dependency.
2026-07-21: Implementation commit 9a8d5d5 and checked-in evidence docs/proof/text-data-wind-tunnel-2026-07-21.md. Minimal adds 38,841 Brotli bytes and 131,072 warm linear-memory bytes: both proposed gates PASS. Complex adds 2,067,685 Brotli bytes and 3,801,088 warm bytes over minimal: both gates FAIL. Actual Parley compiled path still lacks content-digest identity and structured release-mode missing-capability negotiation. Peak transient, allocations, load latency, Wasm throughput, locale, and hyphenation remain NOT_RUN; bead stays in progress and ledger stays Specified.

title: Run resumable-flow and virtual-extent wind tunnel
description: Execute flow-trace-v0 over long-book, million-line, and adversarial continuation corpora before checkpoint encoding or public flow stabilization.
design: Keep checkpoint representation private. Measure page-boundary, fixed-density, and deterministic adaptive policies under the accepted validity, convergence, correction, accessibility, and cancellation laws.
acceptance_criteria: All ADR-0002 traces and density curves have checked-in evidence; every field is exposed by trace diagnostics; accepted gates pass or a human-reviewed exception blocks stabilization.
notes:
2026-07-21: Dependency-free resumable-flow and virtual-extent wind tunnel started on agent/semantic-scene-spine. It will remain private and will not select a public checkpoint serialization or flow API.
2026-07-21: Added dependency-free benches/flow. Synthetic 100,000-block density curves: region p95/max 487/511, fixed16 15/15, fixed64 60/63, fixed256 243/255, adaptive 100/108; adaptive serialized bytes 165,113 are SCREEN, not memory proof. Implemented canonical roundtrip+malformed rejection, strict predecessor restart, successor convergence with zero prefix emission, cancellation with zero publication, million-line extent navigation (<=10 comparisons, <=1024 candidate blocks), correction and accessibility knowledge laws. Adversarial real layout features and calibrated latency remain NOT_RUN/SCREEN.
2026-07-21: Checked-in evidence at docs/proof/flow-wind-tunnel-2026-07-21.md, tied to implementation commit 138a092. Ledger marks layout-scene active but remains Specified; synthetic feature gaps, allocator evidence, latency, and Parley integration explicitly block promotion and bead completion.
2026-07-21 rebase record: the canonical implementation commit is now e1fc9a6 after inheriting governance PR #3; this supersedes the pre-main-rebase identifier 138a092.
2026-07-21: Started the accepted dependency-free adversarial-continuation slice. A small explicit state machine will force float displacement, footnote deferral, table fragmentation, keep relocation, width-dependent object measurement, and nested-flow continuation. Every published checkpoint must resume to the same final state/output digest; cancellation is injected at all six state frontiers plus the pre-publication frontier and must expose zero fragments/checkpoints. This can prove continuation/cancellation laws only; real Parley-backed feature layout remains NOT_RUN.

title: Run position and storage authority wind tunnel
description: Execute the accepted identity-trace-v0 corpus against private canonical-first and collaboration-authority candidates before public storage or position stabilization.
design: Keep candidate representations private and in a separate wind-tunnel crate. Preserve the ADR-0001 fence and stop before adding Loro or another dependency without its explicit gate.
acceptance_criteria: All accepted ADR-0001 corpora, semantic laws, work counters, and quantitative gates have checked-in evidence; failures and unresolved authority choices are explicit; the proof ledger remains honest.
notes:
2026-07-21: Canonical-first private representation wind tunnel authorized by ADR-0001 and started within the first semantic-to-scene campaign. Initial implementation will use no external dependencies and will not stabilize position APIs.
2026-07-21: Added unpublished dependency-free benches/position crate. Implemented six deterministic semantic traces; all pass. Full baseline measurement shows 320-byte estimated label records, 16-byte anchors, 24-byte authored/derived ranges, but correctly fails dense-million work (1,000,000 spans visited) and editor-million persistence (2,000,000 bytes copied); append and collaboration traces remain NOT_RUN. This is a baseline, not candidate selection.
2026-07-21 review correction: million-span corpus now uses one million non-overlapping byte spans with a one-span edit; flat baseline still visits all 1,000,000. Memory and uncalibrated p95 observations are SCREEN, not PASS. Current structural label lower bound is 392 bytes. Snapshot Arc clone is the only quantitative gate marked PASS.
2026-07-21 candidate v1: implemented structurally shared 4 KiB chunked text and 1,024-span blocked range index. Exact pressure now passes editor-million work (4,096 bytes copied, 489 chunk records) and dense-million work (977 block records + 1,024 frontier spans). Differential tests cover 500 deterministic Unicode text edits and 100 overlapping-range edit sequences. The range test exposed cross-block ordering after deletion collapse; fixed by coalescing, sorting, and reblocking only the affected frontier. Append, allocator/confidence, and collaboration gates remain open.
2026-07-21: Checked-in evidence at docs/proof/position-wind-tunnel-2026-07-21.md, tied to implementation commit 85c6500. Ledger marks document-transactions active but remains Specified; exclusions explicitly block promotion and bead completion.
2026-07-21 rebase record: the canonical implementation commit is now 3734c6c after inheriting governance PR #3; this supersedes the pre-main-rebase identifier 85c6500.
2026-07-21: Started the missing dependency-free append-gib slice. The candidate will use an immutable append batch plus a persistent binomial forest so each 64 KiB publication clones/merges only O(log batch-count) metadata, publishes immediately with zero retained unpublished batches, and copies zero source bytes. The one-GiB run may reuse one immutable payload to isolate publication metadata; distinct-payload semantic tests are mandatory, and timing remains SCREEN until the reference-machine/confidence gate exists.
2026-07-21 append candidate v2: implementation commit 9d2b878 adds a persistent binomial append forest. Fifteen tests pass, including 257 distinct payloads across binary carries with an immutable 127-batch snapshot. The exact logical one-GiB run publishes 16,384 shared-payload batches, retains 16,384 leaves, touches/creates at most 30 metadata records per publication, creates 32,767 tree nodes total, reports zero source-byte copies, and retains zero unpublished batches. Recorded publication p95 541 ns remains SCREEN. Because the pressure run reuses one immutable 64-KiB payload, it proves metadata economics only—not one-GiB ingestion, distinct retained payload memory, allocator behavior, or source-to-owned-batch copying. Collaboration authority, node identity, allocator/confidence, and candidate selection remain open.
2026-07-21: Started the next dependency-free identity-trace slice for the accepted tree-anchor laws. Scope is a private semantic model and executable split/join/move/delete traces: split-boundary Before stays on the left while After follows the right; join preserves bias and order; move follows private node identity; delete makes anchors explicitly unresolved. This does not choose a production tree representation, stable node ID, storage encoding, public API, or collaboration behavior.
2026-07-21 tree identity slice: implementation commit a6e2e59 adds a private flat-sibling semantic model and two identity-trace-v0 cases. tree-split-join-anchor PASS digest 4cfe9ce236c34868 proves split boundary side/bias and adjacent join position recovery. tree-move-delete-anchor PASS digest 9f35a489e8912e3a proves a node-relative anchor follows sibling move and becomes explicitly unresolved on delete. All 18 tests pass; the whole semantic suite runs twice and compares trace IDs/digests. Recursive parent splices, subtree moves, schema payloads, concurrent tree operations, compaction, and selective undo remain open; no stable ID or production tree representation is selected.
2026-07-21: Started the accepted dependency-free form-10k slice. The fixed corpus is exactly 10 KiB, 256 authored spans, 64 sparse anchors, and a four-step IME-shaped composition sequence ending in 日本語. The semantic trace will run canonical state and the private chunked/blocked candidates together; the p95 screen will time the final composition transaction through immutable snapshot publication after the first three stages. Timing remains SCREEN without the reference-machine/confidence gate.
2026-07-21 form-10k slice: implementation commit 08f1394 adds the exact 10-KiB/256-span/64-anchor corpus and four active-composition replacements ending in 日本語. Every publication matches canonical text, authored ranges, and expected anchor positions against the private chunked/blocked candidates. form-10k-ime PASS digest 3e56825685aec398; canonical work is 40,979 copied bytes, 256 anchor visits, and 1,024 span visits; candidate work is 16,393 copied bytes, 18 chunk records, 4 block records, and 1,024 span visits. Recorded final-commit-through-snapshot p95 is 15,500 ns over 200 debug samples and remains SCREEN pending a ratified reference machine and confidence protocol. Twenty tests pass.

title: Audit retained Parley seams and upstream path
description: Inventory current Parley analysis, itemization, shaping, break, vertical-text, and inline-object seams and map each Underwood requirement to upstream, adaptation, or an evidence-backed gap.
design: Coordinate with upstream maintainers through written technical questions. Do not fork, patch, pin a production revision, or create public adapter APIs during the audit.
acceptance_criteria: ADR-0004 contains a seam matrix, conformance requirements, upstream issue and PR map, proposed pin policy, patch review threshold, ownership/removal policy, and explicit unresolved decisions.
notes:
2026-07-21: Started decision-support audit from local Parley checkout c6589c625a3cbeda860c64e5322afc4cdb357ea7. Scope remains read-only evidence; no fork, pin, patch, dependency, or public adapter API.
2026-07-21: Decision-support record completed and explicitly approved by Bruce Mitchener. The accepted ADR records source evidence or trace schemas, alternatives, laws, proposed quantitative gates, and remaining implementation-specific gates.
close_reason: Decision-support acceptance criteria satisfied in the checked-in ADR candidate and explicitly approved on 2026-07-21.

title: Audit text-data entry points and distribution budgets

acceptance_criteria: ADR-0003 links exact upstream seams, candidate identity fields, tier inventories, licensing constraints, compressed and resident budget methodology, replay cases, diagnostics, and unresolved human choices.
notes:
2026-07-21: Claimed within the single foundation decision-support campaign defined in PLANS.md. Work is documentation and trace design only; every ratification remains a human gate.
2026-07-21: Decision-support record completed and explicitly approved by Bruce Mitchener. The accepted ADR records source evidence or trace schemas, alternatives, laws, proposed quantitative gates, and remaining implementation-specific gates.
close_reason: Decision-support acceptance criteria satisfied in the checked-in ADR candidate and explicitly approved on 2026-07-21.

acceptance_criteria: ADR-0002 contains trace formats, candidate checkpoint policies, memory/resume metrics, deterministic equivalence tests, failure diagnostics, and decision thresholds sufficient for prototype authorization.
notes:

acceptance_criteria: ADR-0001 names executable trace inputs, outputs, equivalence laws, memory and latency metrics, representative corpora, prototype boundaries, and decision thresholds sufficient to authorize implementation experiments.

2026-07-21: Claimed within the single foundation decision-support campaign defined in PLANS.md. Work is documentation and trace design only; every ratification remains a human gate.
2026-07-21: Decision-support record completed and explicitly approved by Bruce Mitchener. The accepted ADR records source evidence or trace schemas, alternatives, laws, proposed quantitative gates, and remaining implementation-specific gates.

title: Prepare Charter-000 for ratification
description: Turn the open charter proposal into a decision-ready record by naming initial ownership, funding commitments, common-infrastructure boundary, contributor path, and explicit alternatives.
design: Do not infer institutional commitments. Present crisp options and a recommendation, then obtain explicit human ratification in the charter and decision bead.
acceptance_criteria: Every ratification question in Charter-000 has a proposed answer, owner, or explicit unresolved item; alternatives and consequences are complete; human decision is recorded; accepted charter is checked in.
notes:
2026-07-21: Claimed within the single foundation decision-support campaign defined in PLANS.md. Work is documentation and trace design only; every ratification remains a human gate.
2026-07-21: Charter packet completed with ratified flagship, proof vocabulary, WIP discipline, common-infrastructure boundary, funding statement, initial stewardship, explicit specialist vacancies, contributor path, and Overstory fence. Bruce Mitchener explicitly approved the charter.
close_reason: Decision-ready charter completed and explicitly approved by Bruce Mitchener on 2026-07-21.

title: Establish GitHub and durable Beads remote controls
description: Create the forest-rs/underwood remote, configure durable Dolt sync and backup, add CODEOWNERS, and make the checked-in CI jobs required through branch protection and merge queue.
design: Repository-local files are prepared by bootstrap. Remote mutation waits for explicit authority and known owner handles. Preserve the scrubbed JSONL export for review while treating Dolt remote history as authoritative.
acceptance_criteria: Origin exists; Beads remote push and fresh-clone bootstrap are verified; CODEOWNERS names real handles; main protection requires current CI, PRs, conversation resolution, and merge queue; ordinary bypass is disabled; scheduled audit succeeds.
notes:
2026-07-21: Git origin is now public forest-rs/underwood and local beads.role is maintainer. Remote audit: repository has no branches or rulesets yet; Actions enabled with read-only default token, SHA pinning not enforced remotely; vulnerability alerts enabled. No push or remote mutation performed.
2026-07-21: Initial main published. CI run 29794736762 passed all seven jobs. Ruleset 19313566 active: PR-only, strict current-base checks, CODEOWNERS and conversation resolution, squash/linear history, merge queue, no deletion/force push; @waywardmonkeys has pull-request-only steward bypass. Repo is squash-only, auto-merge and branch cleanup enabled, Actions SHA pinning required, workflow token read-only. Beads hooks installed. Durable Dolt remote remains unconfigured because no Dolt credentials or remote namespace are present.
2026-07-21: Added a scheduled and manually dispatchable governance audit that verifies live merge policy, Actions SHA pinning and read-only token permissions, vulnerability alerts, exact protected-main rules, exact required checks, and merge-queue settings. Local execution passes. The workflow-dispatch run remains to be proven after push; durable Dolt remote remains the only external storage gate.
2026-07-21: First main workflow dispatch run 29803019665 exposed the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN administration-read limit. PR #4 (main 1be45a0) now requires UNDERWOOD_GOVERNANCE_TOKEN, gives actionable diagnostics, and documents a fine-grained forest-rs/underwood-only token with Administration: read and explicit expiration. No broad local credential was stored. A human-supplied secret and successful rerun remain gates, alongside the durable Dolt remote.

title: Gate: mandatory pre-foundation decisions ratified
description: Hold the first foundational product campaign until Charter-000 and ADR-0001 through ADR-0004 have been investigated, written, and explicitly ratified.
design: The gate remains open while any mandatory decision child is open. It is closed only by a human after the checked-in charter and ADRs carry their required evidence and explicit decisions.
acceptance_criteria: All five mandatory decision children are closed with checked-in records, alternatives, rationale, evidence plans, and explicit human ratification; unresolved experiments are represented as blocking beads; no foundational public type preceded the gate.
notes: 2026-07-21: Charter-000 and ADR-0001 through ADR-0004 are Accepted with explicit human ratification. No foundational public type preceded the gate. Remaining private proof experiments are represented by und-oh0.10.1.1 through und-oh0.10.1.4; first-slice design is und-oh0.10.1.5.
close_reason: All five mandatory records were explicitly ratified on 2026-07-21; accepted decisions and remaining experiment gates are durable in docs and Beads.

title: First campaign: living document semantic-to-scene spine
description: Drive the first permanent public path from a semantic immutable document through a typed transaction, deterministic projection, explicit text preparation and flow, to a renderer-neutral TextScene consumed by the living agent document wind tunnel.
design: This is not a miniature editor. It establishes only final-architecture boundaries ratified by the mandatory charter and ADRs, includes observability and negative invalidation tests, and uses no private product shortcut.
acceptance_criteria: A public end-to-end path is executable; every created crate has a real role and consumer; position, data, Parley, and flow contracts follow ratified decisions; paint-only and width-only fitness tests prove work not performed; the wind tunnel uses only public contracts; proof ledger advances only with referenced evidence.

title: ADR-0004: Parley boundary and contingency
description:
Ratify the Parley preparation boundary and upstream contingency.

## Decision

Pending human ratification and upstream investigation. The starting architecture isolates all adaptation in underwood_parley and never leaks a fork through the facade.

## Rationale

Underwood needs retained seams without duplicating text physics or allowing upstream review latency to become an accidental architectural veto.

## Alternatives Considered

- Depend permanently on high-level Layout with paint payloads: couples the wrong cache identities.
- Fork Parley broadly: creates an unsustainable second text engine.
- Wait indefinitely for every upstream seam: surrenders product sequencing to external timing.
design: Specify analysis, itemization, shaping and break inputs and outputs; pinned revision policy; upstream contribution path; review threshold; patch ownership and removal; breaker allowance over parley_core; and divergence conformance.
acceptance_criteria: ADR defines the underwood_parley fence, required prepared contracts, version policy, upstream workflow, evidence threshold, patch lifecycle, conformance matrix, prohibited leakage, and human ratification.
notes: 2026-07-21: Bruce Mitchener explicitly ratified the decision record. Acceptance is scoped exactly as documented and does not implicitly authorize a production dependency, unsafe code, a new public API, or an unreviewed workspace boundary.
close_reason: Accepted by Bruce Mitchener on 2026-07-21; durable decision and remaining gates are recorded in the corresponding ADR.

title: ADR-0003: text-data provisioning and identity
description:
Ratify explicit international text-data provisioning and identity.

## Decision

Pending human ratification. The starting architecture uses immutable versioned bundles with deliberate minimal and full tiers.

## Rationale

Unicode, segmentation, line breaking, locale, and hyphenation data affect correctness, caches, replay, and WebAssembly budgets and cannot remain ambient.

## Alternatives Considered

- Baked unversioned data: convenient but nondeterministic across upgrades.
- Inner-loop provider dispatch: flexible but costly and unnecessary.
- Full data everywhere: simplest capability story but unacceptable for constrained targets.
design: Define provider and immutable bundle seam, minimal and full tiers, identity fields, hyphenation ownership, cache and command-replay participation, diagnostics, and compressed and resident budgets.
acceptance_criteria: ADR names all ambient state eliminated, provider and bundle fence, supported tiers, identity and invalidation laws, replay behavior, WebAssembly budgets, failure modes, evidence plan, and human-ratified dependency policy.
notes: 2026-07-21: Bruce Mitchener explicitly ratified the decision record. Acceptance is scoped exactly as documented and does not implicitly authorize a production dependency, unsafe code, a new public API, or an unreviewed workspace boundary.
close_reason: Accepted by Bruce Mitchener on 2026-07-21; durable decision and remaining gates are recorded in the corresponding ADR.

title: ADR-0002: resumable flow and virtual extent contract
description:
Ratify resumable flow and virtual extent semantics.

## Decision

Pending human ratification after long-book and million-line traces. The starting architecture retains versioned checkpoints and honest estimated or measured extents.

## Rationale

Pagination and virtualization require explicit resumable state; eager global flow and undisclosed estimates violate the performance and observability contracts.

## Alternatives Considered

- Eager full flow: simple but globally expensive.
- Opaque implementation checkpoints: cannot support deterministic diagnostics or remote preparation.
- UI-owned scroll correction: duplicates private layout reasoning in hosts.
design: Cover serializable checkpoint fields and versioning, validity, predecessor restart, successor convergence, scheduling, estimates, measured publication, viewport correction, and unrealized accessibility.
acceptance_criteria: ADR includes a long-book edit trace and million-line sparse-measurement trace; defines checkpoint ownership, format and version policy, deterministic resume and convergence laws, correction semantics, failure diagnostics, and human-ratified boundaries.
notes: 2026-07-21: Bruce Mitchener explicitly ratified the decision record. Acceptance is scoped exactly as documented and does not implicitly authorize a production dependency, unsafe code, a new public API, or an unreviewed workspace boundary.
close_reason: Accepted by Bruce Mitchener on 2026-07-21; durable decision and remaining gates are recorded in the corresponding ADR.

title: ADR-0001: position and canonical storage contract
description:
Ratify the position and canonical storage contract.

## Decision

Pending human ratification after the required traces. The starting architecture has three semantic position forms behind a concrete Document facade.

## Rationale

Sparse human identity, dense authored ranges, and dense derived ranges have incompatible persistence and cost contracts; collaboration challenges storage authority.

## Alternatives Considered

- Universal stable anchors: durable but prohibitive for dense layers.
- Universal revision offsets: compact but incorrect for persistent references.
- Generic Document storage parameter: flexible but leaks representation into hot paths and public APIs.
design: Decision requires million-span derived and authored traces plus Loro cursor and mark prototypes. Compare canonical-first and CRDT-authoritative publication without exposing a storage-generic Document.
acceptance_criteria: ADR states position semantics and lifecycle, edit mapping laws, storage fence, adapter boundary, measurable budgets, competing prototype plan, migration implications, and human-approved chosen contract or explicit experiment gate.
notes: 2026-07-21: Bruce Mitchener explicitly ratified the decision record. Acceptance is scoped exactly as documented and does not implicitly authorize a production dependency, unsafe code, a new public API, or an unreviewed workspace boundary.
close_reason: Accepted by Bruce Mitchener on 2026-07-21; durable decision and remaining gates are recorded in the corresponding ADR.

title: Charter-000: spearhead, proof, and stewardship
description:
Ratify the product, proof, and stewardship constitution.

## Decision

Pending human ratification. The starting recommendation is a funded commons anchored by the living agent document as flagship.

## Rationale

The architecture needs a wanted product, literal proof law, concentrated active work, and durable conformance ownership before compatibility promises.

## Alternatives Considered

- Infrastructure-only project: risks adoption without a carrying product.
- Closed or correctness-tiered open core: conflicts with the common-infrastructure intent.
- Unfunded volunteer commons: does not credibly staff sustained platform conformance.
design: Start from the funded-commons anchored by flagship product recommendation. Preserve engine, formats, corpora, and ordinary adapters as common infrastructure while naming how permanent conformance work is supported.
acceptance_criteria: A checked-in charter names goals and non-goals, flagship and wind tunnels, proof promotion law, active campaign discipline, public-API rule, common infrastructure boundary, initial stewardship commitments, and explicit human ratification or recorded overturning of the recommendation.
notes: 2026-07-21: Bruce Mitchener explicitly ratified Charter-000. Initial funding and ownership remain deliberately honest: no secured recurring funding is claimed; bootstrap stewardship is named; specialist vacancies block corresponding proof claims.
close_reason: Accepted by Bruce Mitchener on 2026-07-21; the durable charter records the flagship, proof law, common boundary, stewardship, funding posture, and contributor path.

title: Create the Beads capability and proof graph
description: Represent the complete handover workstreams coarsely in Beads while decomposing only bootstrap and mandatory pre-foundation decisions at execution resolution.
design: The graph uses epics for durable capability fronts, decision beads for forever choices, explicit blockers, and honest proof labels. Future work remains coarse until it reaches the next evidence horizon.
acceptance_criteria: All handover workstreams have a parent epic; Charter-000 and ADR-0001 through ADR-0004 are detailed decision beads; the first executable campaign is blocked by those decisions; bd lint and dependency cycle checks pass; JSONL export is current.
notes:
Completed 29-issue two-resolution graph: ten durable workstream epics, bootstrap tasks, five mandatory decisions under an architectural gate, a blocked first campaign, and next-horizon decision-support beads. bd lint and dependency cycles pass.
Lynx audit fixes: Beads export validation derives the mandatory gate by semantic title rather than hardcoded ID and rejects empty acceptance criteria. Decision beads are blocked by ready decision-support tasks; root is pinned and mandatory gate is explicitly blocked.
close_reason: Beads graph and export checks are semantically robust, linted, acyclic, and expose six deliberate next-horizon tasks.

title: Build the tooling-only policy validator
description: Create the root Cargo workspace and a dependency-free xtask that validates the proof ledger, repository structure, and governance invariants without introducing product crates.
design: xtask is permanent repository tooling, uses std only, has no third-party dependencies, and exposes calm check commands suitable for local use and CI.
acceptance_criteria: Cargo workspace uses the selected MSRV and canonical lints; xtask is fully documented and tested; proof and repository checks fail with actionable diagnostics; fmt, clippy -D warnings, tests, and rustdoc pass.
notes:
Completed dependency-free tooling-only Cargo workspace and xtask validators for proof ledger, crate registry, repository policy, Rust headers, and Beads export. Nine unit tests pass on Rust 1.96 and cargo check passes on Rust 1.92.
Lynx audit fixes: policy now supports future workspace members through the crate registry, synchronizes Rust versions, rejects repository-escaping proof paths, matches CI header years, detects target-specific core dev-dependencies, requires exact no_std attributes, and adds regression tests. Final count: 13 tests.
close_reason: Policy validator Must-fixes and regression tests are complete; stable, MSRV, clippy, tests, docs, and policy checks are green.

title: Install CI and review guardrails
description: Add GitHub Actions and review templates that enforce formatting, linting, tests, documentation, MSRV, Beads export integrity, proof validation, and repository policy.
design: Borrow the forest-rs execution/subduction CI lineage, keep the bootstrap matrix honest for the tooling-only workspace, and make future product-crate feature/no_std jobs explicit rather than pretending they exist now.
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers on pull requests, merge groups, and main pushes; all objective local equivalents pass; action/tool versions are pinned or deliberately documented; PR template requires bead, evidence, proof impact, and human-gate disclosure.
notes: Completed pinned GitHub Actions workflow, scheduled audit, PR template, Dependabot configuration, copyright check, and documented remote controls. Remote execution remains tracked by und-oh0.11.2 because no origin exists.
close_reason: Local CI and review scaffolding is complete with immutable action pins; remote enforcement remains explicitly tracked.

title: Write the Underwood constitution and governance docs
description: Translate the handover and forest-rs tenets into concise repository law: goals, non-goals, ownership fences, Onslaught discipline, Definition of Done, Beads lifecycle, human gates, ADR rules, and proof semantics.
design: AGENTS.md remains operational and scannable; durable explanation lives in docs/CONSTITUTION.md and docs/governance. Rules distinguish permanent vertical slices from stranded incrementalism.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md and governance docs state the complete workflow; goals and non-goals are explicit; every human gate is named; Beads replaces tk consistently; documentation includes a glossary and examples; no product design decision is silently ratified.
notes:
Completed AGENTS.md, executable constitution, governance workflow, open charter, ADR framework, and durable handoff. Decisions remain explicitly unratified. Validation: fmt, Taplo, clippy -D warnings, tests, rustdoc, MSRV, typos, headers, policy, Beads lint/cycles all green.
Lynx audit fix: corrected the documented close/export order so the checked-in Beads export records final state.
close_reason: Constitutional workflow is corrected after adversarial review and final validation is green.

title: Bootstrap the executable constitution
description: Establish the repository constitution, Beads workflow, Cargo policy, CI gates, machine-readable proof ledger, and durable planning graph before foundational product APIs are introduced.
design: Governance is executable: prose states intent; xtask validates structural policy; CI makes objective rules mandatory; Beads orders work; the proof ledger prevents claim inflation. This epic creates no semantic product APIs.
acceptance_criteria: Repository policy is documented and machine-checked; Beads is initialized with the planning graph; CI and local checks are defined; proof entries validate; no production dependency, unsafe code, foundational public API, commit, or push is introduced; all locally runnable validation is green.
close_reason: Bootstrap survived adversarial review and is green with governance, planning, proof, Cargo, CI, and handoff complete.

acceptance_criteria: The capability graph covers every architectural workstream; active work is dependency-ordered; every claimed proof promotion references checked-in evidence; the living agent document uses public Underwood contracts.
